componentDescription: TextInput is the standard single-line text input for forms. It supports labels, hints, error messages, icon slots, password toggling, and size variants. Use TextInput for names, emails, URLs, passwords, and any short free-text input. For multi-line input use TextArea; for numeric input use NumberInput.
features: Left and right slots for icons, buttons, or custom content
Error state with inline error message
Hint text and help icon with hover tooltip
Password visibility toggle via passwordToggle prop
cursor prop for custom cursor styles
textInputGroupPosition for joining multiple inputs visually

usageExamples.title: Email and password login form
usageExamples.description: Two inputs with validation error and password toggle.
usageExamples.code: } />

usageExamples.title: Input with hint text and help tooltip
usageExamples.description: TextInput with sublabel, hint, and a help icon tooltip for extra guidance.
usageExamples.code:
<TextInput
  label="API Key"
  sublabel="Required"
  hintText="Used for authenticating API requests"
  helpIconHintText="You can find your API key in Account Settings > Developer"
  placeholder="sk-..."
  value={apiKey}
  onChange={setApiKey}
  size={TextInputSize.MEDIUM}
/>

value.llmContext: Required. The controlled value of the input. Always manage this with useState. For forms, initialize with empty string '' or the pre-populated field value.

onChange.llmContext: Required. Callback fired with the new string value on every keystroke. Update your state variable here.
onChange.propDescription: Required. Called with new string value on input change

label.llmContext: The field label displayed above the input. Always provide a label for accessibility. Use concise nouns or short phrases (e.g., 'Full name', 'Email address', 'Company').
label.propDescription: Label text displayed above the input

sublabel.llmContext: Secondary label text rendered alongside the main label, typically for optional/required indicators or field type hints (e.g., 'Optional', 'Required').
sublabel.propDescription: Secondary text displayed next to the label

hintText.llmContext: Instructional text displayed below the input. Use for format hints (e.g., 'Must be 8+ characters'), constraints, or context. This is always visible, unlike helpIconHintText which is hover-only.
hintText.propDescription: Always-visible hint text below the input

helpIconHintText.llmContext: Text shown in a tooltip when the user hovers the help icon (?) next to the label. Use for longer explanations, examples, or context that would clutter the form if always visible.
helpIconHintText.propDescription: Tooltip text shown when hovering the help icon

error.llmContext: Set to true to apply error styling (red border, error icon). Pair with errorMessage to show the specific error text. Set based on your validation logic — typically after form submit or on field blur.
error.propDescription: Applies error styling to the input

errorMessage.llmContext: The error text displayed below the input when error=true. Be specific and actionable (e.g., 'Email must include @' not 'Invalid email'). Only shown when error=true.
errorMessage.propDescription: Error message displayed below the input when in error state

leftSlot.llmContext: React node rendered inside the input on the left side. Common uses: search icon, currency symbol, country flag, protocol prefix (http://). Keep it compact — a small icon or short text.
leftSlot.propDescription: React node rendered inside the left side of the input

rightSlot.llmContext: React node rendered inside the input on the right side. Common uses: clear button, copy button, unit label, character count. Use for action affordances or supplementary info.
rightSlot.propDescription: React node rendered inside the right side of the input

passwordToggle.llmContext: Set to true for password fields. Adds an eye icon button to toggle between masked and unmasked text. Always use this for password inputs instead of building custom toggle logic.

size.llmContext: Use SMALL in dense forms, filter bars, or table-inline editing. Use MEDIUM for standard forms. Use LARGE for hero/prominent search fields or onboarding flows.
size.propDescription: Controls input height and font size
